data.title: Why Astro?
data.excerpt: Astro is the modern darling of content sites. Its philosophy aligns precisely with @webhouse/cms — file-based, content-first, zero JS by default.
data.content:
## The Astro audience

Astro became the fastest-growing static-first framework of 2024-2026, powering sites for Block, Cassidoo, Trivago, and many open source docs (including Vercel's documentation). It excels at **content-driven** sites — exactly the @webhouse/cms target.

## Why a perfect fit

- **File-based content** — Astro's content collections expect JSON/MD files in folders. So does @webhouse/cms.
- **Zero JS by default** — Astro ships HTML, hydrates only what needs interactivity. Same philosophy as our static builds.
- **TypeScript native** — type-safe schemas, full IDE support.
- **Server-side rendering** — Astro's SSR mode lets you render dynamic CMS content per request, while static mode pre-renders at build time.

## What's in this example

- `src/lib/webhouse.ts` — TypeScript reader (~110 LOC)
- `src/pages/index.astro` — English home
- `src/pages/da/index.astro` — Danish home
- `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro` — dynamic post route
- `src/layouts/Layout.astro` — shared layout reading globals
- Tiny zero-dep markdown renderer (swap in `marked` for production)

The reader pattern is the same as the other 11 framework consumers — only the framework-specific glue differs.
data.author: Christian Broberg
